mutations.name: createEnterpriseOrganization
mutations.description: Creates an organization as part of an enterprise account. A personal access token used to create an organization is implicitly permitted to update the organization it created, if the organization is part of an enterprise that has SAML enabled or uses Enterprise Managed Users. If the organization is not part of such an enterprise, and instead has SAML enabled for it individually, the token will then require SAML authorization to continue working against that organization.

returnFields.name: clientMutationId
returnFields.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation.

returnFields.name: enterprise
returnFields.description: The enterprise that owns the created organization.

returnFields.name: organization
returnFields.description: The organization that was created.

interfaces.name: EnterpriseAuditEntryData
interfaces.description: Metadata for an audit entry containing enterprise account information.

interfaces.name: TopicAuditEntryData
interfaces.description: Metadata for an audit entry with a topic.

fields.name: topic
fields.description: The name of the topic added to the repository.

fields.name: topicName
fields.description: The name of the topic added to the repository.

enums.name: AuditLogOrderField
enums.description: Properties by which Audit Log connections can be ordered.

values.name: CREATED_AT
values.description: Order audit log entries by timestamp.

enums.name: EnterpriseAdministratorInvitationOrderField
enums.description: Properties by which enterprise administrator invitation connections can be ordered.

values.name: CREATED_AT
values.description: Order enterprise administrator member invitations by creation time.

enums.name: EnterpriseAdministratorRole
enums.description: The possible administrator roles in an enterprise account.

values.name: BILLING_MANAGER
values.description: Represents a billing manager of the enterprise account.

values.name: OWNER
values.description: Represents an owner of the enterprise account.

values.name: UNAFFILIATED
values.description: Unaffiliated member of the enterprise account without an admin role.

enums.name: EnterpriseAllowPrivateRepositoryForkingPolicyValue
enums.description: The possible values for the enterprise allow private repository forking policy value.

values.name: ENTERPRISE_ORGANIZATIONS
values.description: Members can fork a repository to an organization within this enterprise.

values.name: ENTERPRISE_ORGANIZATIONS_USER_ACCOUNTS
values.description: Members can fork a repository to their enterprise-managed user account or an organization inside this enterprise.

values.name: EVERYWHERE
values.description: Members can fork a repository to their user account or an organization, either inside or outside of this enterprise.

values.name: SAME_ORGANIZATION
values.description: Members can fork a repository only within the same organization (intra-org).

values.name: SAME_ORGANIZATION_USER_ACCOUNTS
values.description: Members can fork a repository to their user account or within the same organization.

values.name: USER_ACCOUNTS
values.description: Members can fork a repository to their user account.

enums.name: EnterpriseDefaultRepositoryPermissionSettingValue
enums.description: The possible values for the enterprise base repository permission setting.

values.name: ADMIN
values.description: Organization members will be able to clone, pull, push, and add new collaborators to all organization repositories.

values.name: NONE
values.description: Organization members will only be able to clone and pull public repositories.

values.name: NO_POLICY
values.description: Organizations in the enterprise choose base repository permissions for their members.

values.name: READ
values.description: Organization members will be able to clone and pull all organization repositories.

values.name: WRITE
values.description: Organization members will be able to clone, pull, and push all organization repositories.

enums.name: EnterpriseDisallowedMethodsSettingValue
enums.description: The possible values for an enabled/no policy enterprise setting.

values.name: INSECURE
values.description: The setting prevents insecure 2FA methods from being used by members of the enterprise.

values.name: NO_POLICY
values.description: There is no policy set for preventing insecure 2FA methods from being used by members of the enterprise.

enums.name: EnterpriseEnabledDisabledSettingValue
enums.description: The possible values for an enabled/disabled enterprise setting.

values.name: DISABLED
values.description: The setting is disabled for organizations in the enterprise.

values.name: ENABLED
values.description: The setting is enabled for organizations in the enterprise.

values.name: NO_POLICY
values.description: There is no policy set for organizations in the enterprise.

enums.name: EnterpriseEnabledSettingValue
enums.description: The possible values for an enabled/no policy enterprise setting.

values.name: ENABLED
values.description: The setting is enabled for organizations in the enterprise.

values.name: NO_POLICY
values.description: There is no policy set for organizations in the enterprise.

enums.name: EnterpriseMemberOrderField
enums.description: Properties by which enterprise member connections can be ordered.

values.name: CREATED_AT
values.description: Order enterprise members by creation time.

values.name: LOGIN
values.description: Order enterprise members by login.

enums.name: EnterpriseMembersCanCreateRepositoriesSettingValue
enums.description: The possible values for the enterprise members can create repositories setting.

values.name: ALL
values.description: Members will be able to create public and private repositories.

values.name: DISABLED
values.description: Members will not be able to create public or private repositories.

values.name: NO_POLICY
values.description: Organization owners choose whether to allow members to create repositories.

values.name: PRIVATE
values.description: Members will be able to create only private repositories.

values.name: PUBLIC
values.description: Members will be able to create only public repositories.

enums.name: EnterpriseMembersCanMakePurchasesSettingValue
enums.description: The possible values for the members can make purchases setting.

values.name: DISABLED
values.description: The setting is disabled for organizations in the enterprise.

values.name: ENABLED
values.description: The setting is enabled for organizations in the enterprise.

enums.name: EnterpriseMembershipType
enums.description: The possible values we have for filtering Platform::Objects::User#enterprises.

values.name: ADMIN
values.description: Returns all enterprises in which the user is an admin.

values.name: ALL
values.description: Returns all enterprises in which the user is a member, admin, or billing manager.

values.name: BILLING_MANAGER
values.description: Returns all enterprises in which the user is a billing manager.

values.name: ORG_MEMBERSHIP
values.description: Returns all enterprises in which the user is a member of an org that is owned by the enterprise.

enums.name: EnterpriseOrderField
enums.description: Properties by which enterprise connections can be ordered.

values.name: NAME
